subject: English
chapter: Poems – A Tiger in the Zoo, The Tiger, The Panther

activities_projects.title: Deep Character Analysis of the Tiger
activities_projects.objective: Examine the tiger’s traits, instincts, and symbolic significance.
steps: Read 'The Tiger' thoroughly, noting every description.
Analyze the tiger’s qualities such as power, mystery, and majesty.
Write a detailed paragraph interpreting the tiger as both a literal and symbolic figure.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop critical thinking and literary interpretation skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, textual support, and insightfulness.

activities_projects.title: Symbolism and Metaphor Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Identify and interpret complex symbols and metaphors.
steps: Highlight all metaphors and symbolic phrases in the poem.
Explain how each contributes to the poem’s central themes of power, mystery, and fear.
Present interpretations with textual evidence.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen analytical reading and symbolic interpretation skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification, depth of interpretation, and clarity.

activities_projects.title: Compare the Tiger Across Poems
activities_projects.objective: Contrast the tiger in 'The Tiger' with the tiger in 'A Tiger in the Zoo'.
steps: List traits and emotions of the tiger in each poem.
Analyze differences in freedom, behavior, and tone.
Write a comparative essay highlighting literary techniques used.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance comparative analysis and literary insight.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth, textual references, and clarity of argument.

activities_projects.title: Advanced Mood and Tone Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Explore how language and structure convey mood and tone.
steps: Identify words and phrases that create a sense of awe or fear.
Analyze the poem’s structure (rhyme, rhythm) and how it enhances mood.
Write a detailed explanation connecting tone to reader impact.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand advanced poetic devices and their effects.
activities_projects.evaluation: Analytical depth, examples, and clear explanation.

activities_projects.title: Critical Thinking – Author’s Intent
activities_projects.objective: Explore why William Blake portrays the tiger as he does.
steps: Research historical context of Blake’s writing.
Discuss possible symbolic meanings: divine creation, power, danger.
Write a short essay connecting the tiger’s portrayal to Blake’s philosophy.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will apply critical thinking and literary research skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of historical context, depth of reasoning, and textual support.

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing – Perspective of the Tiger
activities_projects.objective: Express the tiger’s point of view creatively and insightfully.
steps: Write a diary entry or letter from the tiger’s perspective.
Include reflections on power, freedom, and human observation.
Use metaphors and descriptive language inspired by Blake’s style.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen creative writing and literary style skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, depth of perspective, and use of poetic techniques.

activities_projects.title: Literary Device Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Identify and analyze all literary devices used in the poem.
steps: List devices such as alliteration, imagery, metaphor, personification.
Explain how each device highlights the tiger’s traits or themes.
Create a chart linking devices to their effects on the reader.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will deepen understanding of poetic techniques and their functions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, clarity, and depth of analysis.

activities_projects.title: Discussion – Tiger as a Symbol of Power
activities_projects.objective: Engage in higher-order thinking about the tiger’s symbolic role.
steps: Discuss in pairs or groups what the tiger represents (power, awe, danger).
Provide textual evidence supporting different interpretations.
Present conclusions to the class with justification.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop analytical reasoning and discussion skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of argument, evidence, and presentation.

activities_projects.title: Poem Illustration with Symbolism
activities_projects.objective: Creatively visualize the poem while emphasizing symbolic meaning.
steps: Draw the tiger with symbolic elements representing power and awe.
Include details from the poem like fiery eyes or night jungle.
Explain the symbolism in 3–4 sentences.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will merge creativity with literary analysis.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of symbolism, creativity, and explanation clarity.

activities_projects.title: Analytical Essay – Fear and Respect
activities_projects.objective: Explore the duality of fear and respect evoked by the tiger.
steps: Analyze lines that suggest fear, awe, or respect for the tiger.
Explain how these feelings are created through language and structure.
Write a well-structured essay linking textual evidence to interpretation.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will demonstrate critical thinking, analytical writing, and deep literary understanding.
activities_projects.evaluation: Essay coherence, depth of analysis, and textual support.
